name: Chondroitin sulfate
description: Chondroitin sulfate is a sulfated glycosaminoglycan (GAG) composed of a chain of alternating sugars (N-acetylgalactosamine and glucuronic acid). It is usually found attached to proteins as part of a proteoglycan. A chondroitin chain can have over 100 individual sugars, each of which can be sulfated in variable positions and quantities. Chondroitin sulfate is an important structural component of cartilage and provides much of its resistance to compression. Along with glucosamine, chondroitin sulfate has become a widely used dietary supplement for treatment of osteoarthritis. It is also approved and regulated as a symptomatic slow-acting drug for osteoarthritis (SYSADOA) in Europe and some other countries.

interactions.name: Warfarin
interactions.description: Chondroitin sulfate may increase the anticoagulant activities of Warfarin.
